codeReview.summary: Clean, well-structured implementation that meets all feature requirements. Defines all 14 required AST node types (Document, KeyValueNode, MapNode, ArrayNode, StringNode, IntegerNode, FloatNode, BoolNode, DatetimeNode, CommentNode, VariableNode, IncludeNode, BlockStringNode, KeyNode), each embedding NodeBase with Position info. Token/item type enum matches v1 numeric ordering (ItemError=0 through ItemInclude=16) with ItemComment=17 as v2 addition. Node interface with Position() and Type() methods is correctly defined. KeySeparator (equals/colon/space) and CommentStyle (hash/slash) enums are present. All types have Go doc comments. 27 tests cover all types, enums, interface compliance, composite tree structures, and numeric value compatibility. TDD approach was followed correctly.

issues.description: ItemDatetime.String() returns "DateTime" (capital T) while DatetimeNode.Type() in ast.go returns "Datetime" (lowercase t). This naming inconsistency between token type string representation and AST node type string could cause confusion when correlating tokens to nodes in later features. Consider aligning to one casing convention.

String() methods for ItemType, KeySeparator, and CommentStyle panic on unknown values. While this is a common Go enum pattern, it means adding a new enum value without updating the switch will cause a runtime panic rather than a graceful fallback. Consider returning a formatted string like fmt.Sprintf("ItemType(%d)", t) for unknown values, which is what Go's stringer tool generates.

summary: Feature ast-and-token-types passes review. The implementation correctly defines all 14 AST node types, token/item type enum matching v1 values, Position struct, NodeBase, Node interface, KeySeparator and CommentStyle enums. All types have Go doc comments. 27 TDD tests provide thorough coverage. Two non-blocking issues noted: (1) minor "DateTime" vs "Datetime" casing inconsistency between token and AST node string representations, (2) panic on unknown enum values in String() methods. The worker followed the skill procedure correctly with proper TDD workflow, full verification (build, vet, test, v1 baseline), and clean commit.
